learning_outcomes.description: Understand key economic principles as they apply to the tourism sector, including hospitality and events industries
Analyse the options available to tourism, hospitality and event organisations under different market structures
Evaluate the effects of economic conditions and change on tourism, hospitality and event sectors
Interpret the economic contribution and impact of tourism, hospitality and event activities to the broader economy
Communicate evidenced based arguments and strategic recommendations to stakeholders through teamwork

assessments.due_date:
Week 3 Wed - Week 10 Wed

Weekly from Week 3 to Week 10.
assessments.task_description:
The test-bank exercises, consisting of 7 chapters, are designed to assist students with self-practice and self-learning. Students can choose which chapters they want to complete and submit. Multiple attempts per chapter are allowed.

Students will be awarded two (2) points upon completing one chapter (provided more than 80% of the questions have been answered correctly). A maximum of 10 points for this assessment in the final grade is awarded if students complete at least five (5) chapters throughout the semester.

A test bank, consisting of practical questions that integrate economic concepts and tourism issues, is adopted to assist students with self-practice and self-learning. It includes (1) basic questions that allow students to better comprehend the theory and concepts, and (2) advanced applied questions that discuss the impacts of COVID-19, sustainability, and global mega trends on tourism, businesses, and policies.

Students need to engage with the test bank weekly, starting from week 3 to week 10. Each weekly test bank, consisting of 10 multiple-choice questions and two to three open-ended questions, is designed to align with the lecture taught that week. This allows students to review the concepts and test their knowledge by working with the questions. Correct answers will be provided once students submit their responses.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
Week 10, Thu

During the lecture time in Week 10.
assessments.task_description: The In-class exam is scheduled for week 10, including 10-15 multiple-choice questions and 4-5 open-ended questions. It is an in-person exam scheduled during the lecture time. The exam will be invigilated by the lecturer and two tutors.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.assessment_title: Tourism industry economic presentation and team Q&A
assessments.due_date:
Team Presentation Video - 5:00pm - - Week 12 Fri

Group Q&A / An Interactive Oral Assessment (IOA) - During Tutorial - Week 13 Mon - Week 13 Fri
assessments.task_description:
Assessment 3 consists of two components. Part A is a recorded team presentation, and Part B is an interactive oral assessment, in the form of a live Q&A session with your tutor, which will take place during the tutorials in Weeks 13. Further details will be provided on Blackboard.

Part A: Team Presentation Video (35%) (Week 12)

Students work in teams of 4-5 students to apply economic principles to a practical tourism issue. Teams will need to demonstrate (1) how much they have learned from the economic concepts and (2) their ability to apply the economic concepts in addressing issues. This is a significant project and requires substantial work to deliver a satisfactory output.

The output will be submitted as an 8 minutes team video. The recorded file needs to include

  • PPT slides
  • The presenter(s) – each member of the team needs to present


Note: Prior to the teamwork, there will be an in-class session on team dynamics, team appreciation and conflict resolution. Peer evaluation will be used to assess the relative contribution of each member of the team.


Part B: Team Q&A / Interactive Oral Assessment (15%) (Week 13 in-class)

During the Week 13 tutorial, each student will individually participate in an interactive oral assessment by responding to a series of randomly selected questions related to their team's recorded presentation (Part A). The oral assessment is designed to verify each student's understanding of the team's analysis, evaluate their ability to apply relevant economic concepts and justify their recommendations, and confirm their individual contribution to and engagement with the group task.

Note: The Interactive Oral Assessment will be recorded for marking purposes per UQ Policy.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

learning_activities.topic:
Introduction to economics and tourism

This week, we will introduce the fundamentals of tourism economics, explain key economic concepts, explore the tourism system, and examine how economics informs consumer, business, and government decision-making.
learning_activities.topic_description: Introduction to economics and tourism This week, we will introduce the fundamentals of tourism economics, explain key economic concepts, explore the tourism system, and examine how economics informs consumer, business, and government decision-making.

learning_activities.topic:
Demand concepts I (The law demand and elasticity of demand)

Our lecture will explain the law of demand, factors influencing tourism demand, movements and shifts in the demand curve, and the application of elasticity concepts to tourism, hospitality, and event markets.
learning_activities.topic_title: Demand concepts I (The law demand and elasticity of demand)
learning_activities.topic_description: Demand concepts I (The law demand and elasticity of demand) Our lecture will explain the law of demand, factors influencing tourism demand, movements and shifts in the demand curve, and the application of elasticity concepts to tourism, hospitality, and event markets.

learning_activities.topic:
Introduction to assessments

This week's tutorial will provide an overview of the course assessments and expectations, help you connect with your classmates, clarify assessment requirements, and establish groups for the semester's presentation project.
learning_activities.topic_description: Introduction to assessments This week's tutorial will provide an overview of the course assessments and expectations, help you connect with your classmates, clarify assessment requirements, and establish groups for the semester's presentation project.

learning_activities.topic:
Demand Concepts II (Total revenue & Demand modelling and forecasting)

This week, our lecture will explain the relationship between price elasticity and total revenue, and introduce tourism demand modelling and forecasting to support pricing, marketing, and investment decisions in tourism, hospitality, and events.
learning_activities.topic_title: Demand Concepts II (Total revenue & Demand modelling and forecasting)
learning_activities.topic_description: Demand Concepts II (Total revenue & Demand modelling and forecasting) This week, our lecture will explain the relationship between price elasticity and total revenue, and introduce tourism demand modelling and forecasting to support pricing, marketing, and investment decisions in tourism, hospitality, and events.

learning_activities.topic:
Demand curve and elasticity

This week's tutorial will reinforce demand concepts through collaborative exercises and Assessment 1: Test Bank 1, applying the law of demand, elasticity, total revenue, and tourism demand forecasting to analyse real-world tourism scenarios

Ekka Public Holiday - Wednesday 12th August 2026 - Check Blackboard for announcements about affected classes.

learning_activities.topic:
Supply concepts I (Law of supply, Elasticity of supply & Production and costs)

This week, our lecture will explain the law of supply, factors influencing tourism supply, price elasticity of supply, and the role of production, costs, and factors of production in tourism, hospitality, and event businesses.
learning_activities.topic_title: Supply concepts I (Law of supply, Elasticity of supply & Production and costs)
learning_activities.topic_description: Supply concepts I (Law of supply, Elasticity of supply & Production and costs) This week, our lecture will explain the law of supply, factors influencing tourism supply, price elasticity of supply, and the role of production, costs, and factors of production in tourism, hospitality, and event businesses.

learning_activities.topic:
Marginal revenue & Demand forecasting

This week's tutorial will reinforce demand and supply concepts through Assessment 1: Test Bank 2, applying total revenue, elasticity, and tourism demand forecasting to real-world tourism and airline pricing scenarios.
learning_activities.topic_description: Marginal revenue & Demand forecasting This week's tutorial wil l reinforce demand and supply concepts through Assessment 1: Test Bank 2, applying total revenue, elasticity, and tourism demand forecasting to real-world tourism and airline pricing scenarios.

learning_activities.topic:
Supply concepts II (Long run costs & Returns to scale)

This week, our lecture will explain short-run and long-run production costs, profit maximisation, and the role of economies and diseconomies of scale in shaping business expansion decisions in tourism, hospitality, and events.
learning_activities.topic_title: Supply concepts II (Long run costs & Returns to scale)
learning_activities.topic_description: Supply concepts II (Long run costs & Returns to scale) This week, our lecture will explain short-run and long-run production costs, profit maximisation, and the role of economies and diseconomies of scale in shaping business expansion decisions in tourism, hospitality, and events.

learning_activities.topic:
Supply concepts I

This week's tutorial will reinforce supply concepts through Assessment 1: Test Bank 3, applying production costs, supply decisions, and economies of scale to real-world tourism and hospitality case studies.
learning_activities.topic_description: Supply concepts I This week's tutorial will reinforce supply concepts through Assessment 1: Test Bank 3, applying production costs, supply decisions, and economies of scale to real-world tourism and hospitality case studies.

learning_activities.topic:
Market equilibrium

This week, our lecture will explain how market equilibrium is determined through the interaction of demand and supply, and examine how macroeconomic indicators—including GDP, commodity prices, interest rates, and exchange rates—influence tourism demand, supply, and market outcomes.
learning_activities.topic_description: Market equilibrium This week, our lecture will explain how market equilibrium is determined through the interaction of demand and supply, and examine how macroeconomic indicators—including GDP, commodity prices, interest rates, and exchange rates—influence tourism demand, supply, and market outcomes.

learning_activities.topic:
Supply concepts II

This week's tutorial explores tourism supply and cost concepts by applying cost analysis, economies of scale, diminishing marginal returns, and marginal decision-making principles to determine the profit-maximising output of tourism and hospitality businesses, while supporting preparation for Assessment 1 through Test Bank 4.
learning_activities.topic_description: Supply concepts II This week's tutorial explores tourism supply and cost concepts by applying cost analysis, economies of scale, diminishing marginal returns, and marginal decision-making principles to determine the profit-maximising output of tourism and hospitality businesses, while supporting preparation for Assessment 1 through Test Bank 4.

learning_activities.topic:
Market structure and strategies

This week's lecture examines tourism market structures and competitive strategies by analysing pricing, product differentiation, cost leadership, strategic alliances, and business growth strategies.
learning_activities.topic_description: Market structure and strategies This week's lecture examines tourism market structures and competitive strategies by analysing pricing, product differentiation, cost leadership, strategic alliances, and business growth strategies.

learning_activities.topic:
Market equilibrium

This week's tutorial applies market equilibrium concepts and evaluates how changes in GDP, interest rates, exchange rates, and commodity prices influence tourism markets, while preparing students for Assessment 1 through Test Bank 5.
learning_activities.topic_description: Market equilibrium This week's tutorial applies market equilibrium concepts and evaluates how changes in GDP, interest rates, exchange rates, and commodity prices influence tourism markets, while preparing students for Assessment 1 through Test Bank 5.

learning_activities.topic:
Economic impact of tourism

This week's lecture explores the economic contribution of tourism by analysing Tourism Satellite Accounts (TSA), interpreting key tourism economic indicators, and evaluating tourism's contribution to GDP, employment, exports, and regional development.

learning_activities.topic:
Market structure and pricing

This week's tutorial applies market structure concepts and competitive strategies through case studies and problem-solving activities on product differentiation, cost leadership, vertical integration, and pricing strategies, while supporting preparation for Assessment 1 through Test Bank 6.
learning_activities.topic_description: Market structure and pricing This week's tutorial applies market structure concepts and competitive strategies through case studies and problem-solving activities on product differentiation, cost leadership, vertical integration, and pricing strategies, while supporting preparation for Assessment 1 through Test Bank 6.

Teamwork dynamics + Exam review

The Employability Team will deliver a guest lecture and interactive workshop on teamwork dynamics to strengthen students' collaboration, communication, and conflict management skills for group projects. This will be followed by an Assessment 2 exam review and consultation session. As important assessment and employability skills will be covered, all students are strongly encouraged to attend.

Economic impact of tourism

This week's tutorial develops students' ability to interpret Tourism Satellite Account (TSA) data by analysing tourism's direct economic contribution at the national and regional levels, while supporting preparation for Assessment 1 through Test Bank 7.

learning_activities.topic:
Economic assessment of events

This week's lecture applies economic evaluation methods for events by analysing direct in-scope expenditure, return on investment, economic impact assessment, and cost–benefit analysis to evaluate the economic value of tourism and special events.

Review the in-class exam

learning_activities.topic:
Tourism, environment and climate change

This week's lecture applies economic evaluation methods to assess the costs and benefits of tourism and special events through case studies, while examining market failure, environmental externalities, and the tourism carbon footprint to support sustainable decision-making.
learning_activities.topic_description: Tourism, environment and climate change This week's lecture applies economic evaluation methods to assess the costs and benefits of tourism and special events through case studies, while examining market failure, environmental externalities, and the tourism carbon footprint to support sustainable decision-making.

learning_activities.topic:
Economic assessment of events

This week's tutorial applies economic evaluation techniques to analyse the costs and benefits of special events through real-world case studies, with a focus on Direct In-scope Expenditure (DISE), Return on Investment (ROI), Economic Impact Assessment (EIA), and Cost–Benefit Analysis (CBA).
learning_activities.topic_description: Economic assessment of events This week's tutorial applies economic evaluation techniques to analyse the costs and benefits of special events through real-world case studies, with a focus on Direct In-scope Expenditure (DISE), Return on Investment (ROI), Economic Impact Assessment (EIA), and Cost–Benefit Analysis (CBA).

learning_activities.topic:
Course review & summary

This final lecture explores strategies for mitigating tourism-related carbon emissions through the roles of governments, businesses, and tourists. The session concludes by reviewing the key economic concepts and learning outcomes developed throughout the course, highlighting how students have applied these principles in their Assessment 3 presentations to address contemporary tourism challenges.

learning_activities.topic:
Assignment 3 (Part B - Team Q&A/Interactive oral assessment)

Assessment 3 comprises two components. Part A is a recorded team presentation, which must be submitted in Week 12. Part B is an interactive oral assessment (Team Q&A) conducted as a live question-and-answer session with your tutor during the Week 13 tutorial.

During the oral assessment, each student will individually respond to a series of randomly selected questions related to their team's recorded presentation (Part A). The purpose of the oral assessment is to verify each student's understanding of the team's analysis, assess their ability to apply economic concepts and justify their recommendations, and ensure individual engagement with the group task.
